findings.title: Manual fallback breaks the empty-target contract
findings.detail: `emitManual()` always appends `posixQuote(target)`, so `specflow-launch-watch ""` tells the user to run `specflow-watch ''`. The proposal explicitly requires the empty-target case to preserve `specflow-watch`'s no-argument branch-resolution behavior. Omit the extra token when `target === ""`, and add a regression test for the empty-target manual fallback.

findings.title: Required dispatcher branch coverage is missing
findings.detail: The proposal requires stubbed-PATH tests that cover each dispatcher branch, but this suite never exercises `tmux`, `screen`, `osascript`, `x-terminal-emulator`, `konsole`, or `alacritty`. That leaves the original macOS `osascript` regression and the sync branches unverified, so the acceptance criteria are not met. Add per-branch tests that assert the expected argv/cwd behavior.

findings.title: Launcher coverage misses required dispatcher and cwd scenarios
findings.detail: The proposal explicitly called for unit coverage of each dispatcher branch plus git-root and non-git cwd resolution. The new suite covers manual fallback, `$TERMINAL`, `gnome-terminal`, `xfce4-terminal`, `kitty`, `wezterm`, `xterm`, and one fallthrough case, but it does not exercise `tmux`, `screen`, `osascript`, `x-terminal-emulator`, `alacritty`, or assert the repo-root / `process.cwd()` cwd behavior that the spec makes part of the launcher contract. Add stubbed-PATH tests for those branches and cwd semantics.

findings.title: Template lint only scans registered command templates, not every assets/commands template
findings.detail: The new build-time guard is wired to `contracts.commands.map(c => c.body.templatePath)`, so it only checks templates that are currently referenced by command contracts. The accepted command-template-authoring spec says the build must reject forbidden `$1..$9` / `$ARGUMENTS` placeholders in any `.md.tmpl` under `assets/commands/`. As written, an unregistered template in that directory bypasses the lint entirely. Enumerate `assets/commands/*.md.tmpl` directly (or equivalent) and add a test proving an unregistered template still fails the build.

findings.title: Watch guide still references the removed `_try_bg` helper
findings.detail: After switching the guide to `specflow-launch-watch`, the per-emulator table still says `wezterm` and `xterm` use `_try_bg`. That helper no longer exists once the inline Bash dispatcher is removed, so the guide no longer accurately reflects the new CLI contract. Update those rows to the new liveness-probe wording and keep the snapshot aligned.

findings.title: Templates now depend on a launcher that was never implemented
findings.detail: The spec’s main requirement is a new `specflow-launch-watch` CLI with the dispatcher, stdout, manual-fallback, and always-exit-0 contract. This diff updates the templates and `package.json` to call/export that command, but there is no new launcher implementation in the diff. As written, `/specflow` and `/specflow.watch` would replace the broken inline helper with a missing binary. Add the new CLI and wire it into the build/distribution path before switching the templates over.

findings.title: Required launcher test coverage is missing
findings.detail: The proposal explicitly calls for unit tests covering dispatcher precedence, empty-target handling, and manual fallback for `specflow-launch-watch`. No such test file or equivalent coverage was added here; the only test-related changes are snapshot updates. The cross-platform launcher is non-trivial behavior and needs direct tests before this can be accepted.
